news.title:
FMCG majors Nestle, HUL, Godrej Consumer, and Dabur face Q4 pressure due to crude price hikes and supply chain issues.
Nestle, HUL, Godrej, and Dabur report varying growth, with factors like palm oil, crude, and Amla prices impacting margins.
news.summary:
FMCG majors Nestle, HUL, Godrej Consumer, and Dabur face Q4 pressure due to crude price hikes and supply chain issues.

news.title:
Consumer goods firms like Dabur, Marico, DMart, V Mart saw sales rebound in March quarter.
GST rationalisation in Sept 2022 boosted volumes for AWL Agri Business and others.
news.summary:
Consumer goods firms like Dabur, Marico, DMart, V Mart saw sales rebound in March quarter.

news.title:
Nomura reaffirms 'Buy' on six stocks, including Hindustan Aeronautics, Adani Ports, Dabur, Marico, FSN E-Commerce, and Godrej Consumer, with 24-62% upside.
Key drivers include strong order pipelines, demand recovery, margin resilience, and diversified business operations across sectors.
news.summary:
Nomura reaffirms 'Buy' on six stocks, including Hindustan Aeronautics, Adani Ports, Dabur, Marico, FSN E-Commerce, and Godrej Consumer, with 24-62% upside.
Key drivers include strong order pipelines, demand recovery, margin resilience, and diversified business operations across sectors.

news.title:
Morgan Stanley rates Dabur India Underweight; target price ₹412, citing 6% YoY EBITDA growth and 4Q revenue growth.
Domestic demand improves sequentially, but geopolitical tensions in Middle East create cautious near-term outlook for Dabur.
news.summary:
Morgan Stanley rates Dabur India Underweight; target price ₹412, citing 6% YoY EBITDA growth and 4Q revenue growth.

news.title: Goldman Sachs retains a neutral rating on Dabur with a target of 490.

news.title:
Brokerages issued fresh ratings: Buy on TVS Motor, Marico, Adani Ports, Sobha; Underweight on Dabur.
Key drivers: Dabur's Q4 EBITDA growth, Marico's food segment strength, and TVS Motor's pricing power.
news.summary:

news.title:
Dabur India Ltd's Q4 FY26 saw strong domestic growth, offsetting challenges in Middle East markets due to geopolitical issues.
Key brands like Dabur Amla, Vatika, and Real Juices drove growth, with consolidated revenues expected to grow mid-single digits.
news.summary:
Dabur India Ltd's Q4 FY26 saw strong domestic growth, offsetting challenges in Middle East markets due to geopolitical issues.
Key brands like Dabur Amla, Vatika, and Real Juices drove growth, with consolidated revenues expected to grow mid-single digits.

news.title:
Axis Securities warns of margin pressure on HUL, Dabur, Asian Paints due to rising crude and input costs.
FMCG, paints, QSR, and retail sectors face cost shocks; Jubilant FoodWorks, Trent, and V-Mart most vulnerable.
news.summary:

news.title:
Dabur India's domestic business showed steady momentum; FMCG to see high single-digit growth.
International markets face challenges with low single-digit growth; consolidated revenue growth at mid single digits.

news.title:

news.title:
Rising input costs, West Asia conflict, and weak rupee pressure FMCG margins; durable goods firms plan price hikes.
FMCG firms to stagger price hikes starting with 3-4% in April; urban FMCG value growth contracts by 4-5% in March.
news.summary:
Rising input costs, West Asia conflict, and weak rupee pressure FMCG margins; durable goods firms plan price hikes.

news.title:
Dabur India Ltd announces NCLT-directed meeting on May 2, 2026, for unsecured creditors.
Meeting aims to consider and approve the Scheme of Amalgamation with Sesa Care Pvt Ltd.
news.summary:
